text:
I'll search for the exact cited title and authors to verify this reference.
Based on my web search results, I have found critical information about this reference.

**Key finding:** The cited reference has the **exact title** "TOMG-Bench: Evaluating LLMs on Text-based Open Molecule Generation" and the arXiv ID 2412.14642 is correct. However, the cited author list is **incomplete and missing two authors**.

Multiple authoritative sources confirm the actual author list:
- The official GitHub repository and README file show the complete author list as: "Jiatong Li*, Junxian Li*, Yunqing Liu, Changmeng Zheng, Xiao-Yong Wei, Dongzhan Zhou, and Qing Li"
- The arXiv paper v1 shows: "Jiatong Li, Junxian Li, Yunqing Liu, Dongzhan Zhou, Qing Li"
- Later versions (v3) on the project website cite: "Li, Jiatong and Li, Junxian and Liu, Yunqing and Zheng, Changmeng and Wei, Xiaoyong and Zhou, Dongzhan and Li, Qing"

The cited reference omits **Weida Wang** (or **Weida Wang** in v2/v3) and **Changmeng Zheng**, and **Xiaoyong Wei**. The GitHub citation format in the repository specifically shows these additional authors.

This is a case where the core paper is real with the correct title and correct core authors, but the author list is **incomplete and incompletely cited**. The cited authors (Jiatong Li, Junxian Li, Yunqing Liu, Dongzhan Zhou, Qing Li) do appear on the actual paper, but there are additional authors missing, which represents an inaccurate bibliographic citation.

EXPLANATION: The paper with the cited title exists at the correct arXiv URL, but the author list is incomplete—it omits Weida Wang, Changmeng Zheng, and Xiaoyong Wei who appear as co-authors in the official repository and later versions. This incomplete author attribution is a citation error.
LINK: https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.14642
FOUND_TITLE: TOMG-Bench: Evaluating LLMs on Text-based Open Molecule Generation
FOUND_AUTHORS: Jiatong Li, Junxian Li, Weida Wang, Yunqing Liu, Changmeng Zheng, Xiaoyong Wei, Dongzhan Zhou, Qing Li
FOUND_YEAR: 2024
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
